# Start

> Your coding assistant.

Start is a macOS desktop app for working with coding agents in one focused workspace. It helps you ask questions about a codebase, attach project context, edit files, review diffs, and prepare code for shipping.

The app is local-first. Project files, prompts, chat history, indexes, logs, API keys, and access tokens stay on the user's Mac. Start does not include tracking pixels, advertising identifiers, or third-party telemetry.

## Core surfaces

- Chat sessions for asking coding questions and directing agent work
- Parallel work across multiple conversations
- Multi-model selection for matching the model to the task
- File context so the assistant can understand the code you choose
- File edits with reviewable changes
- Diff viewer for inspecting updates before committing work
- Git workflow support for repository status, diffs, commits, and pull requests

## Local data

- Application data is stored locally on the Mac
- API keys and access tokens are stored in the macOS Keychain
- AI prompts go directly from the user's machine to the provider they configure
- Connected services, such as Git hosts, are contacted directly from the user's machine
- Crash logs and diagnostics are not transmitted automatically

## Requirements

- macOS desktop app
- Git for repository workflows
- A configured AI provider or compatible agent backend
- Project dependencies installed by the user when commands need them

## Website pages

- `/`: landing page
- GitHub releases: https://github.com/sasicodes/start/releases
- `/privacy`: privacy policy
- `/terms`: terms of use

- App name: Start

- Tagline: Your coding assistant.
